task: Research ActMem and M2A dual-layer memory papers

corrections.40_percent_claim: NOT FOUND in either paper. Largest gain in M2A is ~23% relative (Qwen3-VL-8B backbone). Largest ActMem gain is +14.78% retrieval vs LightMem.
corrections.actmem_layers: ActMem does NOT use episodic/semantic layers. It uses a single unified KG with two edge types (semantic + causal).

ActMem.title: ActMem: Bridging the Gap Between Memory Retrieval and Reasoning in LLM Agents
ActMem.key_idea: The retrieval-reasoning gap: NaiveRAG achieves 84.86% retrieval accuracy but only 61.54% QA accuracy. Finding facts does not equal reasoning about them. ActMem closes this via a causal+semantic knowledge graph with two-pass counterfactual retrieval.

architecture.type: Unified causal+semantic knowledge graph (single layer, not episodic/semantic split)
modules: Fact Extraction: LLM converts dialogue to atomic subject-predicate-object sentences
Fact Clustering: single-pass incremental cosine clustering (threshold tau=0.2)
KG Construction: semantic edges (cos>0.3) + causal edges (PMI filtering tau=0.8)
Counterfactual Retrieval: vector search → LLM generates counterfactual consequences → second retrieval pass
architecture.key_innovation: Counterfactual reasoning allows deducing implicit constraints (e.g. 'user moved cities → gym membership cancelled')

ActMem.vs_flat_memory: NaiveRAG (flat vector) has highest retrieval accuracy (84.86%) but lowest QA (61.54%). ActMem trades retrieval precision for large QA gains via reasoning.

M2A.title: M2A: Multimodal Memory Agent with Dual-Layer Hybrid Memory for Long-Term Personalized Interactions
M2A.key_idea: True dual-layer memory for multimodal long-term personalization. Memory co-evolves with the user. Only system evaluated that handles both text and images in memory.

two_agents.ChatAgent: ReAct loop: Query memory → Generate response → Update memory
two_agents.MemoryManager: Dedicated read-write controller, isolates memory ops from conversation context

layer_1_RawMessageStore.description: What-happened layer: append-only, immutable, chronological log
layer_1_RawMessageStore.content: Complete turns including text, images, video frames

layer_2_SemanticMemoryStore.description: What-it-means layer: compressed high-level knowledge entries
layer_2_SemanticMemoryStore.content: Text description + auto-generated image captions + associated images
layer_2_SemanticMemoryStore.key_field: evidence_ids: pointers back to specific RawMessageStore ranges

retrieval.type: Tri-path hybrid fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60)
retrieval.path_1: Dense cosine similarity on text embeddings
retrieval.path_2: BM25 sparse keyword matching (handles exact terms, names, dates)
retrieval.path_3: Cross-modal image retrieval (text query vs image captions)
retrieval.progressive_narrowing: Broad semantic retrieval → fine-grained raw-message lookup via evidence_ids

lancedb_application_insights: 1. ADD EVIDENCE POINTERS: Semantic memory entries should carry evidence_ids linking back to raw observation records. Our current system lacks this link between compressed knowledge and source observations.

2. TWO-AGENT ISOLATION: Separating memory read/write from conversation context (M2A's MemoryManager pattern) maps to our gate/session separation. Memory retrieval results should not overflow conversation context.

3. BM25 + DENSE FUSION (RRF): Our current FTS5 + vector search should be fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) rather than independent fallback. This handles exact-match queries (names, dates) that pure semantic search misses.
4. PMI FILTERING FOR CAUSAL CHAINS: Before storing causal relationships in knowledge entries, score them with Pointwise Mutual Information to filter spurious causality (ActMem uses tau=0.8).
5. SINGLE-PASS INCREMENTAL CLUSTERING: Group related facts by cosine similarity (tau=0.2) before KG insertion. Avoids full reindex per conversation turn.
6. COUNTERFACTUAL RETRIEVAL PASS: After initial vector retrieval, generate 'what would follow from these facts' and run a second retrieval pass. Highest-value addition for reasoning tasks.
7. PROGRESSIVE NARROWING (HIGHEST VALUE PER M2A ABLATION): Semantic layer retrieval first, then drill into raw records via pointers. Removing this causes the largest single accuracy drop (-16 pts).
